reasoning: Iteration 1: Initial query generation. Learner has identified critical optimizations: (1) Parquet row group pruning on sorted l_shipdate column (2-83% data skipped per query), (2) column projection (50-75% I/O reduction), (3) parallel row group scanning, (4) hash joins on smaller sides (Q3), and (5) fused scan/filter/aggregate pipelines. These strategies leverage the verified Parquet file layout where lineitem is sorted by l_shipdate.

query_guidance.Q1: Single-table aggregation on lineitem with date filter. CRITICAL: (1) Push l_shipdate <= '1998-09-02' to Parquet reader with row group pruning (skip last ~2% of 600 row groups based on sorted l_shipdate). (2) Project only 8 of 16 columns: l_returnflag, l_linestatus, l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_tax, l_shipdate for 50% I/O reduction. (3) Convert decimal128(15,2) types to double early at scan level. (4) Hash aggregate on (l_returnflag, l_linestatus) expecting ~4 groups. (5) Partition 600 row groups across threads for parallel scanning (morsel-driven parallelism). (6) Fuse scan→filter→aggregate into single pipeline over RecordBatches. Use Arrow compute kernels for Sum/Mean aggregations.
query_guidance.Q3: Multi-table join: customer ⋈ orders ⋈ lineitem, then aggregate and top-K. JOIN ORDER: (1) Build hash table on customer filtered by c_mktsegment='BUILDING' (~300K rows), probe with orders filtered by o_orderdate < '1995-03-15'. (2) Build hash table on join result (~3M rows, project only o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority), probe with lineitem filtered by l_shipdate > '1995-03-15'. PARQUET PUSHDOWN: (1) Push l_shipdate > '1995-03-15' to lineitem Parquet reader with row group pruning (skip first ~38% of 600 row groups, ~230 RGs). (2) Push c_mktsegment equality and o_orderdate filters. COLUMN PROJECTION: customer (2 of 8 cols, 75% reduction), orders (4 of 9 cols, 56% reduction), lineitem (4 of 16 cols, 75% reduction). PARALLELISM: Build hash tables in parallel, partition lineitem's ~370 qualifying row groups across threads. AGGREGATION: GROUP BY l_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority with SUM(l_extendedprice * (1-l_discount)). TOP-K: Use heap-based partial sort for LIMIT 10 instead of full sort.
query_guidance.Q6: Single-table scan of lineitem with highly selective filters. CRITICAL: (1) Push l_shipdate range ['1994-01-01', '1995-01-01') to Parquet reader with row group pruning (read only ~100 of 600 row groups, ~17% of data, based on sorted l_shipdate). (2) Project only 4 of 16 columns: l_shipdate, l_discount, l_quantity, l_extendedprice for 75% I/O reduction. (3) Apply filters in selectivity order: l_shipdate (Parquet level, ~17%), then l_discount BETWEEN 0.05 AND 0.07 (~2% overall), then l_quantity < 24 (~1% overall). (4) Use Arrow compute::Filter for vectorized filtering. (5) Fuse filter+compute+aggregate into single pass (no intermediate materialization). (6) Partition ~100 qualifying row groups across threads, each with local sum accumulator. (7) Convert decimals to double early. Result is single aggregate: SUM(l_extendedprice * l_discount).
